steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The previous sentence establishes that isolation came soon after discovery for many elements — but not all. The blank opens a new clause: "____ the isolation process took years," with radium as the example. The blank must signal that this was true for only some elements, not all.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'However': signals direct contrast. The contrast was already introduced in the prior sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Thus': signals a logical conclusion drawn from prior evidence. Nothing here is being concluded. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Moreover': signals an addition of a parallel point. This is a qualification, not an addition. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Sometimes': signals that the pattern (years-long isolation) held in some but not all cases — exactly what the radium example illustrates. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
